paragraphs.text: Hello, my name is Rasmus. I'll try to be short and to the point. I have an instance of where i bought an older game, to get into the nostalgia of my youth. "Far cry 2" published by ubisoft, I bought it a couple of years back but didn't touch it until recently. I reinstalled it and tried it again prior to this message. I loaded up my past save, where i was stuck in a room with 2 npc's after the introduction. Game over. I tried starting a new game aswell. I went through the introduction this time aswell, found myself stuck in the same room. But this time because I didn't follow the instructions step by step. I went up to a healing station and used it before I was prompted to do so, so It had no effect when I tried after being told to. Game over. I would have tried again, if I knew for certain that I would have a full and enjoyable game experience after that point. But honestly I had no hopes left, when I had first struggled with the mouse sensitivity. I had to max out the mouse sensitivity. otherwise it wasn't any good flow too it, there's a slight delay before it actually registers the movement and then it only moves vertically, horizontally or diagonally. The delay should be enough of a disability in a shooter game. I was surprised I got it to work this time as I struggled with it the last time. Last point which i never got to confirm. Is that during the introduction or drive through the starting areas. There were npc on the way that was unnaturally "jumping" as if someone just dragged their characters up and down as they still were doing their npc behaviours. This to me is what really stands out the most, as it's a shooter game. This is nothing like it was like playing pre 2010. To me it's a clear sabotage of the game, that either the devs or someone with access that have altered this in order to make it unplayable even if you can get the game up and "running". Thank you for your time, energy and patience
